sourceMovie.title: G.I. Jane
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: G.I. Jane works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Lone Survivor
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If G.I. Jane clicked for you, Lone Survivor (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after G.I. Jane?
faq.answer: Try Lone Survivor, Greyhound, Tears of the Sun—each shares DNA with G.I. Jane while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is G.I. Jane?
faq.answer: G.I. Jane (1997) is best described as Action, Drama, War. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is G.I. Jane worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
